prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: project-bound RPG world atlas map, portrait orientation
Primary request: Warhammer 40,000 / Dark Imperium inspired grimdark planetary war-zone atlas for the terrain type dual_continent.
Scene/backdrop: two major continents on a polluted Imperial war world, separated by uneven toxic seas and narrow straits, hive city mega-structures, underhive slums, manufactorum belts, industrial wastelands, promethium refinery zones, ruined battle sectors, orbital landing fields, gothic cathedral-spires and mechanicus noosphere overlays.
Style/medium: high-resolution grimdark far-future atlas, Imperial cartography, gothic sci-fi armor-panel border, toxic ochre atmosphere, mechanicus technical overlay, battle-worn parchment-metal map.
Composition/framing: tall portrait map, asymmetric continents of different sizes; the toxic sea separation is diagonal and S-shaped with C-shaped gulfs, L-shaped channels and jagged straits, not a simple vertical divider; include auspex compass, severe gothic border, supply routes, hazard glyphs, hive icons and tactical sector boundaries without readable text.
Geography constraints: realistic complex coastlines with peninsulas, bays, gulfs, capes, broken shores, curved coasts, deltas, polluted estuaries, inland seas, ash cliffs and varied landforms; span rad deserts, toxic swamps, hive sprawl, polar wastes, mountain fortress zones, industrial basins, ash wastes and blasted battlefields.
Avoid: clean bright sci-fi, symmetrical continents, modern satellite image, readable labels, UI, watermark, logo, real-world map copy.
